page: UpCloud HIMEM-8xCPU-192GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8 physical cores with 1 thread per core, backed by 192.0 GB of system memory, which yields a high resource density of 24.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 400 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between compute capacity and memory allocation. While the single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% according to stress-ng benchmarks, multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average performance for small-block cached reads, but drop to weak for large-block uncached reads and poor for large-block uncached writes. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, consistently perform in the average tier.

This server configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory footprints relative to compute power, avoiding the expense of higher vCPU tiers. The high RAM-to-vCPU ratio makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and database operations that do not require intensive single-core processing speeds.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-8xCPU-192GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz, 192.0 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD. The hardware delivers a high resource density of 24.0 GB of memory per core. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance and database operations, though single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write bandwidth are limited, placing in the bottom 10%. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand high single-threaded compute performance. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications.
